toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run these tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, the standard approach is to use environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dumps
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

These environment variables tell the .NET runtime to automatically generate a dump when the process crashes.


Now I'll execute the tests with these environment variables configured.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-591f2ce3bdab4e488acdcf2648235734 && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-591f2ce3bdab4e488acdcf2648235734/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command to run with crash dump collection:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="./crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**What these environment variables do:**

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` | Enables automatic dump on crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` | Full memory dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | Output path (`%p` = process ID) |

If a test crashes the host process, a `.dmp` file will be written to the project directory. No crashes occurred this run, so no dump was generated.
